title: Two Sum II — Input Array Is Sorted
description:
Given a **1-indexed** array `numbers` that is already sorted in non-decreasing
order, find two numbers that add up to a specific `target`.

Return the indices of the two numbers, `index1` and `index2`, **added by one**,
as `[index1, index2]`. There is exactly one solution, and you may not use the
same element twice.
constraints: numbers is sorted in non-decreasing order

solutions.summary: Try every pair of indices until one sums to the target.

steps.title: Start from the function shape
steps.explanation: Begin with the signature and a default `[]` return so the function is valid.

steps.title: Loop over every pair
steps.explanation: Walk index `i`, and for each `i` walk a later index `j`, so each pair is seen once.

steps.title: Return the matching pair (1-indexed)
steps.explanation: When the two values add up to `target`, return their indices plus one.

solutions.summary: Shrink a window from both ends using the sorted order.

steps.title: Start from the function shape
steps.explanation: Same starting point: the signature plus a default `[]` return.

steps.title: Put a pointer at each end
steps.explanation: Because the array is sorted, start `lo` at the smallest value and `hi` at the largest.

steps.title: Move the pointers toward each other
steps.explanation: Loop while `lo < hi`, comparing the current sum to the `target` next.

steps.title: Decide which pointer to move
steps.explanation: If the sum matches, return both indices (+1). If it is too small, raise `lo`; otherwise lower `hi`.
